1HiTeXeR renders the Menger sponge as a solid black silhouette with no color, no holes/cutouts, and no 3D structure visible. The entire surface coloring, 3D depth, and fractal hole structure are completely missing from the HiTeXeR render.
2HiTeXeR renders the Menger sponge as a completely black silhouette with no color, no 3D depth, and no visible internal structure/holes. The reference shows a detailed 3D Menger sponge with full Rainbow palette coloring (red/orange/yellow/green/blue gradient), visible holes throughout the structure, and proper 3D rendering of the fractal cube geometry.
3HiTeXeR renders the Menger sponge as a flat 2D hexagonal projection (orthographic top-down view) rather than the correct 3D isometric cube view. The reference shows a 3D cube with visible top, left, and right faces with deep fractal holes, while HiTeXeR shows a flat hexagonal shape with no 3D depth or visible cube faces.
4HiTeXeR renders a flat hexagonal projection with no depth or holes visible - it looks like a filled hexagon with color gradients. The reference render shows a 3D Menger sponge fractal cube with proper perspective, shading on three visible faces (top, left, right), and the characteristic recursive square holes throughout the structure. The 3D projection and depth rendering are completely missing in HiTeXeR.
5The HiTeXeR render shows a flat 2D hexagonal pattern without any 3D depth, whereas the reference shows a proper 3D Menger sponge cube with visible holes/tunnels and dark shadows giving full depth. The 3D projection and surface rendering is completely broken in HiTeXeR - all faces appear to be rendered as flat colored tiles without proper 3D geometry or hidden-surface removal.

#Issue Identified by Sonnet
1The labels are rendering as plain text 'extnormalSl...' (truncated/cutoff) instead of properly formatted math text 'extnormalSlope = N'. The HiTeXeR render shows labels in a large italic serif font that are clipped/cut off at the right edge, while the reference shows the full label text including the '= value' portion. Additionally, the HiTeXeR canvas appears to be too small or the labels are too large, causing them to overflow and get clipped, whereas the reference shows all labels fitting within the image bounds.
2The labels are rendering incorrectly in HiTeXeR: instead of showing 'Slope = X' with the equals sign and numeric value, the text is cut off showing only 'extnormalSl' (truncated). The '\textnormal' command is not being processed correctly, and the label content after the opening brace is missing. In the reference, full labels like 'extnormalSlope = 2' are shown (with the textnormal font rendering), while HiTeXeR shows only a truncated prefix.
3The labels are rendering incorrectly in HiTeXeR: instead of showing the full text 'Slope = X' with proper formatting, they show only 'extnormalSl...' (truncated, raw command text). The labels appear in a large italic/script font and are cut off, whereas the reference shows properly rendered text like 'extnormalSlope = 2' etc. Also, the HiTeXeR image is zoomed in/cropped differently, showing only the left half of the diagram while the reference shows the full diagram with all labels visible on the right side.
4The labels are rendered incorrectly in HiTeXeR: instead of showing formatted math like 'Slope = 1', the labels show only 'extnormalSl...' (truncated), indicating that the \textnormal{} LaTeX command inside the math labels is not being processed correctly and the text is rendering as literal command text rather than the intended formatted output.
5The labels are rendering incorrectly in HiTeXeR: the `\textnormal{...}` command is not being processed, causing the text to appear in italic/math font as 'extnormalSl...' (truncated) instead of the upright roman font 'Slope=...' shown in the reference. Additionally, the labels are being cut off and appear much larger relative to the diagram, with the full label text not visible.

Visual Fix Cycles

#Issue Identified by Sonnet
1The HiTeXeR render shows a plain blue 3D rectangular box/prism shape, completely missing the mathematical formula content. The reference render shows a 3D-extruded version of the mathematical equation (integral from -infinity to +infinity of e^(-alpha*x^2) dx = sqrt(pi/alpha)) rendered in blue with proper 3D depth. HiTeXeR failed to render the texpath() font/glyph geometry and instead produced a simple rectangular solid.
2HiTeXeR renders a plain blue 3D rectangular solid (box), completely missing the mathematical formula text. The reference shows the LaTeX formula '∫_{-∞}^{+∞} e^{-αx²} dx = √(π/α)' extruded in 3D in blue. The texpath() function (which converts LaTeX text to a path) is not being rendered, so instead of the extruded text formula, only a featureless rectangular block appears.
3HiTeXeR renders a plain blue 3D rectangular box/bar shape instead of the 3D-extruded mathematical formula. The reference image shows the integral equation extruded into a 3D blue shape with the actual text characters visible and recognizable. HiTeXeR completely fails to render the text path (texpath) into meaningful character shapes, instead producing a featureless rectangular solid.
4HiTeXeR renders a plain 3D blue rectangular box/prism instead of the 3D extruded mathematical formula. The reference image shows a 3D extrusion of the integral equation (∫e^(-αx²)dx = √(π/α)) rendered as a solid blue 3D object with visible math text, while HiTeXeR completely fails to render the text path and instead shows only a featureless blue cuboid.
5HiTeXeR renders a plain blue 3D rectangular box/prism, completely failing to render the mathematical formula text. The reference shows a 3D extruded version of the integral equation (∫e^{-αx²}dx = √(π/α)) with the mathematical characters visible as a 3D blue shape. HiTeXeR appears to be using a fallback that replaces the texpath() text with a simple rectangle.

The path closure and curve direction of the 'center' guide are not being interpreted correctly.
5The filled black region (filldraw of the 'center' guide closed path) is completely wrong in HiTeXeR. In the reference, it forms the correct S-shaped yin-yang black half covering the entire right side. In HiTeXeR, it renders as a distorted shape — a roughly rectangular/triangular block extending to the right edge of the image — indicating the path construction for 'center{E}..{N}(1,0)..{W}cycle' is being computed incorrectly, likely due to wrong direction vectors or cycle closure.

#Issue Identified by Sonnet

3In the HiTeXeR render, the label '$F_{\mathrm{centrifugal}}$' is positioned directly above and overlapping with the '$F_{\mathrm{Earth}}$' label, causing them to crowd together in the right portion of the diagram. In the reference render, '$F_{\mathrm{centrifugal}}$' is centered above the short arrow at (.25, .04) and '$F_{\mathrm{Earth}}$' is clearly separated below it to the right — the labels are distinctly placed with no overlap.
4The short centrifugal arrow (drawn from (0,0.04) to (0.5,0.04)) is missing from the HiTeXeR render. In the reference, there is a small red arrow above the main line pointing right toward the F_Earth arrow, but in the HiTeXeR version this small arrow does not appear.
5The label placement is wrong in HiTeXeR: 'F_sun' and 'F_Earth' labels appear below the arrows (at the arrowhead/tail ends), while in the reference they are centered above their respective arrows.
